The question appears to be based on common business studies or economic statistics regarding the longevity of corporations. While life expectancy varies by industry and era, many academic texts and business studies cite an average lifespan for firms in the range of 20 to 30 years. Given the options provided, 20 or 30 are the most frequent academic answers, but 20 is often used in general business contexts to describe the volatility of modern firms.

\boxed{B}
